message:
feat(ansi): implement escape sequence generation and buffered output

This commit adds the ANSI escape sequence layer responsible for
generating terminal control codes and efficiently writing them to
stdout with state tracking to minimize redundant output.

src/ansi/mod.rs - Module Exports:
- ColorMode enum: TrueColor, Color256, Color16
- Re-exports: sequences module, AnsiWriter
- Color mode detection from terminal capabilities

src/ansi/sequences.rs - Escape Sequence Generation:
Cursor Control:
- move_to(row, col): \x1b[{row};{col}H (1-based)
- move_up/down/left/right(n): Relative movement
- save_cursor(), restore_cursor(): DEC private sequences
- show_cursor(), hide_cursor(): Visibility control
- set_cursor_style(): Block, underline, bar with blink option

Color Sequences:
- True color (24-bit): \x1b[38;2;r;g;bm (fg), \x1b[48;2;r;g;bm (bg)
- 256 color: \x1b[38;5;nm, \x1b[48;5;nm
- 16 color: \x1b[30-37m (normal), \x1b[90-97m (bright)
- reset_colors(): \x1b[0m

Text Attributes:
- All 8 attributes: bold, dim, italic, underline, blink, inverse,
  hidden, strikethrough with corresponding enable/disable sequences

Screen Management:
- enter_alt_screen(), exit_alt_screen(): Alternate buffer
- clear_screen(): \x1b[2J
- clear_line(): \x1b[2K
- scroll_up/down(n): Scroll region

Mouse Support:
- enable_mouse_tracking(), disable_mouse_tracking()
- SGR mode preferred for extended coordinates

Hyperlinks (OSC 8):
- start_hyperlink(id, url): \x1b]8;id={id};{url}\x1b\\
- end_hyperlink(): \x1b]8;;\x1b\\

Synchronized Output:
- begin_sync(): \x1b[?2026h - Start buffering
- end_sync(): \x1b[?2026l - Flush buffer atomically
- Eliminates flicker on supported terminals

src/ansi/output.rs - Buffered ANSI Writer:
AnsiWriter<W: Write>:
- 8KB internal buffer for batched writes
- State tracking to minimize output:
  - current_fg/bg: Only emit color changes when different
  - current_attrs: Track active text attributes
  - current_link: Track active hyperlink
  - cursor_row/col: Cache position for optimal moves
- Delta encoding: Only emits what changed since last write
- Cursor optimization: Calculates byte cost of relative vs absolute
  moves to choose the shorter sequence
- flush() writes buffer to underlying writer
- reset_state() for full state refresh

Performance Considerations:
- Buffer batches many small writes into larger syscalls
- State tracking avoids redundant escape sequences
- Cursor move optimization reduces output bytes
- Synchronized output prevents visual tearing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
